prompt: Implement an optimistic event publisher in TypeScript for a Nostr client. When a user creates an event: (1) show it immediately in the UI state with a 'pending' status, (2) publish to multiple relays, (3) track OK responses from each relay, (4) parse OK message reason prefixes to distinguish success (including 'duplicate:' as success), retriable errors (rate-limited, auth-required), and permanent failures (blocked, restricted), (5) update the event status to 'confirmed' on first success or 'failed' if all relays reject, (6) implement a timeout for relays that don't respond. Also implement event deduplication that handles both regular events (by id) and replaceable events (by pubkey+kind, keeping latest created_at with lowest id as tiebreaker). Save to outputs/optimistic-publisher.ts.
assertions: Events are added to local state with a 'pending' status before being sent to relays
OK responses are tracked per-relay per-event (not just a single boolean)
The 'duplicate:' OK reason prefix is treated as success (not failure)
Retriable error prefixes (rate-limited, auth-required) are distinguished from permanent failures (blocked, restricted)
Event status transitions to 'confirmed' when at least one relay accepts
Event status transitions to 'failed' only when ALL relays have rejected or timed out
A timeout mechanism exists for relays that don't send OK responses
Regular event deduplication uses event.id with a Set or Map
Replaceable event deduplication keeps the latest created_at, and breaks ties by keeping the lowest event id
